verdict_reasoning: LAMP2 is a single-pass transmembrane protein resident in the lysosomal membrane. During lysosomal exocytosis—triggered by immune cell activation (T cell / NK cell degranulation)—the lysosomal membrane fuses with the plasma membrane, exposing LAMP2 on the cell surface via its own TM anchor. The CD107b designation and HGNC gene-group membership confirm surface biology. Surface presence is documented but context-dependent on cell-state activation.
